Utility functions for calculating used and free heap memory in an application.

# Purpose
This C header file provides utility functions for memory management within an application. It includes two functions: [`heap_bytes_used`](<#heap_bytes_used>) and [`heap_bytes_free`](<#heap_bytes_free>). The [`heap_bytes_used`](<#heap_bytes_used>) function returns the number of bytes of heap memory currently in use by the application. The [`heap_bytes_free`](<#heap_bytes_free>) function returns the number of bytes of heap memory that are not in use. These functions are part of the Memory Management module, which is a subset of the Foundation group, as indicated by the documentation comments.
# Imports and Dependencies

---
- `stddef.h`


# Function Declarations (Public API)

---
### heap\_bytes\_used<!-- {{#callable_declaration:heap_bytes_used}} -->
[View Source →](<../../../../../src/fw/applib/app_heap_util.h#L30>)

Calculates the number of bytes of heap memory currently being used.
- **Description**: Use this function to determine the current usage of heap memory by the application. It is useful for monitoring memory consumption and ensuring that the application does not exceed memory limits. This function does not require any parameters and can be called at any time to get the current heap usage.
- **Inputs**: None
- **Output**: Returns the number of bytes of heap memory currently in use as a `size_t` value.
- **See Also**: [`heap_bytes_used`](<app_heap_util.c.md#heap_bytes_used>)  (Implementation)
